task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the specified requirements and handles an edge case correctly.
trace: To implement the `median` function, I first check if the input list is empty and return `None` if it is, handling the edge case of an empty list. Then, I sort the list to arrange the numbers in ascending order. Depending on whether the length of the sorted list is odd or even, I calculate the median accordingly: for an even number of elements, I take the average of the two middle numbers; for an odd number, I return the middle number directly as a float. This solution ensures correctness and readability while addressing the edge case of empty input.
